author: @SchwaAlien
text: Seems like this would complement a solar thermal plant for night-time / cloudy day generation, especially if there was a way to run it in a liquid closed loop and consume any low grade heat waste in the system when it’s sunny.  There wouldn’t be big issues with long term storage of the energy or limited charge-discharge cycles; technically there may be efficiency issues but as long as the system could be large enough and remain economical it wouldn’t be a big deal since the primary energy input isn’t costing anything, it just needs to be able to have the capacity, operate for a long time and do virtually infinite cycles.

replies.author: @hootsmin
replies.text:
"in 1878, the physicist James Clerk Maxwell wrote in a book review for Nature:

The truth of the second law is ... a statistical, not a mathematical, truth, for it depends on the fact that the bodies we deal with consist of millions of molecules... Hence the second law of thermodynamics is continually being violated, and that to a considerable extent, in any sufficiently small group of molecules belonging to a real body."

author: @mikaelfransson3658
text: This I think is the best efficient done Sterling yet a gamma variant appr. 40% efficiency! I read somewere the max is 80% efficiency on a Sterling! it got me to think! A hole cirkel no Fysikal work just only heat and cool with two flyweels in an equilibrium! the configuration for this is 4 Alpha-stirlingsmotors in a Boxermotor with 8 cylinders! this motor was made in 1950-1960, (now only 6 cylinders Boxermotors is made) by Porsche and some airplane-engins! What to do? Just do four converted Sterling Alphas in a same 8-cylinders Boxermotor! Now you get all on the flyweels if this has propellers in the middle the cooling effects is intergraded and a generator om both flyweels! To think boxer its easier to have a v-flat stroke on the cylinders! I have never seen this yet! But it might be time for this thing to be made! Then this typ of fuel can be enought! With higher pressure and lighter gas (helium) i think 80% efficiency Stirling is in the reachable target! Have a nice evening Robert and thanks fore a good  video!

replies.author: @Enngel0
replies.text:
The heating agent and responsible reaction vary from product to product. Calcium oxide is used in the following reaction:

CaO(s)+ H2O(l) → Ca(OH)2(s)
Copper sulphate and powdered zinc can also be used, but this process is less efficient:

CuSO4(s) + Zn(s) → ZnSO4(s) + Cu(s)
Anhydrous calcium chloride is often used as well. In this case, no chemical reaction occurs, instead the heat of solution is generated.

Commercial heat sources for self-heating food packaging use an exothermic (heat releasing) reaction, for which there are several common formulations. These include:

Quicklime aka calcium oxide, and water. Quicklime, inexpensive and readily available, is generally recognized by the FDA as safe.[2] The product of the reaction is calcium hydroxide.
Finely powdered magnesium metal alloyed with a small amount of iron, and table salt, actuated by adding water

author: @billschas9202
text: Interesting concept, Rob.  The idea of concentrating ambient heat and running a Stirling engine has interested me for quite a while.  I come from Texas, where summer heat gets close to 100F most days in the summer.  We have huge parking lots (acres of parking spaces) that are invariably asphalt, and by 3 pm June - September, the heat coming off the black top at the shopping center is painful.  It seems reasonable that a system of heat sinks and heat exchangers could run Stirling engines large enough to produce usable energy.

author: @aadhithvarathan5432
text: This is amazing. Surely we can power a Stirling Engine using any exothermic reaction.

replies.author: @jsoswell
replies.text: Even an endothermic reaction could run a sterling engine. It runs on heat differential. So creating a cold area could work just as well if it is colder enough to create the heat differential that is necessary to make the medium inside the chamber expand and contract to move the piston.

replies.author: @aadhithvarathan5432
replies.text: @jsoswell Of course

replies.author: @aadhithvarathan5432
replies.text: Even better

replies.author: @jsoswell
replies.text: The principle to remember is whether endo or exothermic, the greater the heat differential, the more horsepower the engine will have.
